summary: DHH announces that 37signals is shifting its default developer platform from Mac to Linux after over twenty years of Apple monoculture. He frames this as aligning the company's tools with its open-source values while gaining practical benefits like better understanding Windows-using customers. The change is a default, not a mandate — mobile developers still need Macs, and personal preferences are respected. DHH emphasizes supporting companies like Framework that represent the independent future he wants to see, while acknowledging Apple still makes great hardware.
key_points: After 20+ years of Mac monoculture, 37signals is making Linux the default for developers and system operators
The shift aligns the company's tooling with its open-source values and reduces dependency on Apple's ecosystem
Defaults matter even when they're not mandates — they set institutional tone and direct spending toward preferred vendors like Framework
Running Mac, Windows, and Linux across the company better reflects how customers actually use their products
The move is partly motivated by growing philosophical disagreements with Apple's direction on computing freedom
money_quotes: My entire career has been spent in the service and sun of open source, both as a contributor and a beneficiary, and closing the loop with a desktop operating system is very satisfying.
I love voting with my dollars for more of a future I'd like to see, and Framework represents just that.
Apple has evolved into a company that's much harder to recommend for people who care about the future of computing, but they still make great hardware.
None of this has to be binary. Hate/love. Yes/no.
Going forward, we'll spend more of our money and attention on platforms that align better with the independence and freedom we so cherish in all other aspects of our business.
The year of Linux on the desktop. Who would have thought!
key_insight: A company's default tools should reflect its values, and for an open-source-driven shop increasingly at odds with Apple's closed ecosystem, Linux is the natural realignment.
